metadata.purpose: Initial public/course parameter seed for stage-two sanity checks. Values are not final aircraft design data.

course_z11_hover_example.notes: Course hover homework/example parameter set. Use for hover trim and power-scale checks.

course_z11_hover_trim_result.notes: Course hover trim output. Use as a direction and magnitude check for the first trim solver, not as target-aircraft data.

z10_public_low_estimate.notes: Deagel lists 12 m main rotor diameter, 5100 kg empty weight, 7000 kg max takeoff weight and 3200 hp takeoff power. Tip speed is still an assumed value for first-pass hover checks.

z10_public_high_estimate.source: Secondary public specifications sometimes report 13 m rotor diameter and 8 t class mass; keep as sensitivity case only. Reference pages: https://www.army-technology.com/projects/z-10-attack-helicopter/ and https://www.militarytoday.com/helicopters/z10.htm
z10_public_high_estimate.notes: Uses 13 m rotor diameter and 8 t class mass as a high public estimate.

d6075_vrs_reference_model.source: Wayne Johnson, NASA TP-2005-213477, Table 5 D6075 helicopter model.
d6075_vrs_reference_model.notes: Use as VRS reference rotor geometry, not as the project target aircraft.

airfoil_seed.reason: Publicly available symmetric airfoil data for first-pass blade-element sanity checks when actual rotor airfoil data is unavailable.

vrs_seed_thresholds.source: FAA Helicopter Flying Handbook Chapter 11 and Wayne Johnson NASA TP-2005-213477.
vrs_seed_thresholds.notes: Draft engineering trigger based on FAA VRS entry conditions: powered vertical or near-vertical descent, at least 300 ft/min descent, and airspeed below effective translational lift. The 12 m/s horizontal-speed gate is a conservative first-pass ETL-scale value; FAA demonstration text also cites less than 10 kt for training entry. Replace with calibrated Johnson boundary or test data when available.
